description_long: This object is a historical-style cannon mounted on a wooden wheeled carriage. The cannon barrel is made of dark, heavy metal, featuring a flared muzzle and several reinforcing rings along its length. The carriage is constructed from robust wooden planks, reinforced with metal brackets and bolts, and is equipped with four solid wooden wheels for mobility. Metal rings and chains are attached to the sides of the carriage, likely for maneuvering or securing the cannon. The overall design is utilitarian, with visible wear and a matte finish, suggesting it is built for durability rather than aesthetics. The object exudes a sense of weight and solidity, typical of artillery pieces used in historical urban fortifications or military displays.
description: A heavy, dark metal cannon mounted on a sturdy wooden wheeled carriage with metal reinforcements and side chains. The design is robust and utilitarian, evoking historical artillery.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular muzzle of the cannon and the symmetrical arrangement of the wooden carriage and wheels.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full length of the cannon barrel, the wooden carriage, and the attached metal chains and rings.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the breech of the cannon and the back structure of the wooden carriage.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirroring view 1, showing the cannon barrel, carriage, and side details.
attribute_weapon_mounts: One fixed large-caliber cannon barrel mounted centrally on the wooden carriage.
attribute_armor_plating: None visible; the object consists of exposed metal and wood with no additional armor plating.
attribute_camouflage_pattern: None visible; the object is finished in solid black and brown tones.
